title: SLO Compliance Check
description: Given a series of request outcome lines (1=success, 0=failure), determine if the service meets its SLO target. Compute the success rate, whether the SLO is met, and the remaining error budget as a percentage. Error budget remaining = (1 - slo_target) * 100 - actual_error_rate_percent.
constraints: success_rate and error_budget_remaining are rounded to 1 decimal place
met_slo is True if success_rate >= slo_target * 100

examples.explanation: 4 successes out of 5 = 80% success rate. With SLO=99.9%, error budget = 0.1%. Actual error rate = 20%, so remaining = 0.1 - 20.0 = -19.9% (budget exhausted).

starter_code.python:
def check_slo(log_content: str, slo_target: float = 0.999) -> dict:
    pass

test_cases.description: 80% success rate far below 99.9% SLO, budget exhausted
100% success rate meets SLO with budget remaining
90% success rate (1 failure in 10) exceeds error budget
